Docs
Curated reviewer and trust docs

System rails

ZK: privacy review and proof anchors
REFHE: confidential settlement and payout posture
MagicBlock: execution corridor for responsive paths
Hosted reads: operational speed, diagnostics, and runtime readiness
QVAC: local-first intelligence before signing
Anchor 1: current Testnet program and IDL posture
Private Payments API
Challenge/login
On-chain receipts
MagicBlock track
On-chain private payment corridor for MagicBlock execution

This route exposes MagicBlock as an execution lane: live Solana RPC proof for the corridor, finalized private payment receipts, challenge initiation, bearer-token private reads, and wallet-signed continuation in one place.

Open governOpen executeOpen curated docs
Route summary

Use PrivateDAO like infrastructure, not like a static brochure

This services route explains hosted reads, privacy policies, payout corridors, and the integration starter needed to plug the product into real organizations.

Use PrivateDAO like infrastructure, not like a static brochure
Frontier protocol spine

MagicBlock is bound to the same visitor-repeatable on-chain claim layer

The corridor keeps its native MagicBlock proof endpoint and finalized Solana Testnet receipts, while every visitor can create a fresh PDAO_ENCRYPTED_CLAIM_V1 Memo claim from the browser and verify the signature on Explorer. Building and development continue without interruption as PER delegation evidence is deepened.

Verify protocol spinePrepare MagicBlock claim
Reviewer route

MagicBlock proof starts on-chain, then protects private reads

The public proof endpoint checks the corridor PDA and finalized Testnet receipts live through Solana RPC. Private balances remain behind MagicBlock challenge/login, then the user wallet signs and submits any payment transaction to the connection returned by the payments API.

Open Umbra payout laneOpen Cloak laneOpen proof
MagicBlock on-chain corridor

Private payments are tied to explorer-visible Testnet receipts

PrivateDAO exposes the MagicBlock corridor as a live proof lane: the read-node verifies the corridor PDA and the deposit, private transfer, withdraw, settle, and execute signatures directly against Solana RPC. Private balance reads still require MagicBlock challenge/login, so sensitive state remains wallet-authorized.

Receipts
reading
finalized on Testnet
Corridor
reading
MagicBlock PDA
Runtime
reading
Solana Testnet proof
Open MagicBlock live probeOn-chain proofHealthChallengePayments API
Execution shape
1base-layer corridor account
2MagicBlock private deposit
3private transfer receipt
4withdraw receipt
5corridor settlement
6governed execute receipt
7wallet-signed private reads through challenge/login
PrivateDAO is confidential coordination infrastructure for organizations on Solana, connecting private operations to verifiable outcomes.
Developed in public through github.com/X-PACT/PrivateDAO with product proof, runtime evidence, and final security hardening moving in parallel.

© PrivateDAO. Core source remains open where the repository license applies. Brand, product design, documentation, digital evidence, and official deployments are reserved; official-looking derivative deployments, service packaging, or commercial use require written coordination through official PrivateDAO channels.
